tips.title: Use the Pomodoro Technique
tips.description: Study for 25 minutes, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 cycles, take a longer 15-30 minute break.

tips.title: Prioritize Your Tasks
tips.description: Use the Eisenhower Matrix: categorize tasks by urgency and importance. Focus on important tasks first.

tips.title: Create a Study Schedule
tips.description: Plan your study sessions in advance. Consistency helps build productive habits.

tips.title: Practice Deep Breathing
tips.description: When stressed, try the 4-7-8 breathing technique: breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8.

tips.title: Take Regular Breaks
tips.description: Your brain needs rest to consolidate information. Short breaks improve focus and retention.

tips.title: Exercise Regularly
tips.description: Physical activity reduces stress hormones and improves mood. Even a 10-minute walk helps.

tips.title: Get Enough Sleep
tips.description: Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep. Sleep is crucial for memory consolidation and cognitive function.

tips.title: Eliminate Distractions
tips.description: Turn off notifications, use website blockers, and create a dedicated study space.

tips.title: Use Active Recall
tips.description: Test yourself regularly instead of just re-reading. This strengthens memory pathways.

tips.title: Study in Short Bursts
tips.description: Your brain retains information better in 20-50 minute sessions rather than marathon study sessions.

tips.title: Use the Feynman Technique
tips.description: Explain concepts in simple terms as if teaching someone else. This reveals gaps in understanding.

tips.title: Embrace Mistakes
tips.description: Mistakes are learning opportunities. They help your brain form stronger connections.

tips.title: Practice Self-Compassion
tips.description: Be kind to yourself. Negative self-talk increases stress and reduces performance.

tips.title: Set Realistic Goals
tips.description: Break large goals into smaller, achievable milestones. Celebrate small wins.

tips.title: Visualize Success
tips.description: Spend a few minutes visualizing yourself succeeding. This primes your brain for achievement.

tips.title: Stay Hydrated
tips.description: Dehydration impairs cognitive function. Keep water nearby while studying.

tips.title: Eat Brain-Boosting Foods
tips.description: Include nuts, berries, fish, and dark chocolate in your diet for better brain function.

tips.title: Limit Caffeine
tips.description: While caffeine can help focus, too much causes anxiety and disrupts sleep. Moderate your intake.

tips.title: Use Spaced Repetition
tips.description: Review material at increasing intervals. This is proven to improve long-term retention.

tips.title: Create Mind Maps
tips.description: Visual organization helps you see connections between concepts and improves recall.

tips.title: Teach Others
tips.description: Explaining concepts to peers reinforces your own understanding and reveals knowledge gaps.

tips.title: Use Multiple Resources
tips.description: Different perspectives on the same topic deepen understanding and make learning more engaging.

tips.title: Optimize Your Study Space
tips.description: Good lighting, comfortable temperature, and minimal clutter improve focus and productivity.

tips.title: Use Background Music Wisely
tips.description: Instrumental music or nature sounds can help some people focus. Experiment to find what works for you.

tips.title: Join Study Groups
tips.description: Collaborative learning provides motivation, different perspectives, and accountability.

tips.title: Talk to Someone
tips.description: When feeling overwhelmed, talk to friends, family, or a counselor. You're not alone.

tips.title: Make Time for Hobbies
tips.description: Engaging in activities you enjoy reduces stress and prevents burnout.

tips.title: Practice Gratitude
tips.description: Write down three things you're grateful for each day. This improves mood and reduces stress.

tips.title: Start Early
tips.description: Cramming is ineffective. Start reviewing material weeks before exams for better retention.

tips.title: Practice Past Papers
tips.description: Familiarize yourself with exam format and question types. This reduces anxiety and improves performance.
